nativeRange.habitat: Rocky subtidal reef environments, where it grows attached to hard substrate within the photic zone (the depth range that receives enough light to support photosynthesis).

imageCandidates.notes: Lucas Rodrigues, 1542x2048. In-situ tidepool shot on rock; whole plant in frame, sharp, vibrant reddish-red fleshy branching blades - best represents natural habitat and diagnostic coloration.
Lucas Rodrigues, 1542x2048. Whole thallus held in hand against water; sharp, vibrant crimson, clearly shows fleshy elongated tongue-shaped blades.
Lucas Rodrigues, 1542x2048. Tighter in-hand close-up of blade tips; sharp and vibrant but a cropped detail view, kept as non-recommended extra.

summary: The Serpent Tongue is a specialty ornamental red macroalgae (Halymenia elongata) valued for its fleshy, reddish-pink, elongated blades. Related to Dragon's Breath and similar Halymenia species, it is notably sensitive to shipping stress.
careNotes:
The Serpent Tongue (Halymenia elongata) belongs to family Halymeniaceae, order Halymeniales — the same family as its close relatives Dragon's Breath (H. durvillei), Pink Dragon's Breath (H. floresia), Dragon's Tongue (H. dilatata), Leafy Ogo (H. maculata), and Orange Galaxy (H. stipitata). It produces fleshy, thin, delicate blades that are cylindrical in form and reddish-pink in color, giving it the elongated tongue shape reflected in its common name. The Serpent Tongue is a rare specialty species in the aquarium hobby, available in cutting sizes of roughly 2–2.5" and 5" or larger. It is reported to be VERY sensitive to shipping stress, and live arrival guarantees from known suppliers are typically restricted to overnight delivery services only.

Standard reef parameters are generally advisable: temperature approximately 22–27°C, pH 7.8–8.4, salinity SG 1.023–1.025, and alkalinity 8–12 dKH. Recommended nutrient targets are NO3 5–20 ppm and PO4 0.02–0.1 ppm, with calcium around 400–450 ppm and magnesium around 1,250–1,350 ppm. Medium lighting is generally advised for Halymenia species to maintain coloration without browning; full-spectrum LED is preferred over actinic-only. Flow should be gentle to moderate — comparable to a soft-coral display tank — as excessive flow has been known to tear delicate red macroalgae blades.

As a red alga, Serpent Tongue must NOT be planted in the sand bed; burying the base of the thallus (the algal body) will cause it to melt. It should instead be attached to live rock using superglue, zip-ties or fishing line, or by wedging it into a crevice. Weekly dosing of iron, iodine, and trace elements is generally recommended for ornamental red macroalgae to support coloration. Propagation in the aquarium hobby is primarily by fragmentation. The Serpent Tongue is generally considered reef-safe, but like all Halymenia it is palatable to herbivorous fish; keeping it away from tangs, rabbitfish, and sea urchins is generally advisable. Growth rate for ornamental Halymenia species in this blade form tends to be medium.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros — Serpent Tongue
primary.notes: Sole hobby trade source found for this species. Confirmed scientific name Halymenia elongata; reddish-pink fleshy cylindrical blades; VERY sensitive to shipping (Next Day Air required for live arrival guarantee); 2–2.5" and 5"+ cutting sizes offered. No specific water parameters provided.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros — Macroalgae Care Guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source. Provided parameter targets: temp 22–26°C (72–78°F), pH 8.1–8.4, Ca 400–450 ppm, Mg 1250–1350 ppm, NO3 5–20 ppm, PO4 0.02–0.1 ppm. Confirmed red algae must not be buried in sand (melt warning). Confirmed attachment methods: superglue, zip-ties/fishing line, wedging.
